title: Weak Cryptographic Hash
description: Use of MD5 or SHA-1 for data integrity or digital signatures violates SOC2 CC6.7 (transmission protection) and CC6.6 (logical access). These algorithms are broken for cryptographic integrity purposes.

finding.message: Weak cryptographic hash '{{token}}' used in {{file}} at line {{line}}.

references: Replace MD5/SHA-1 with SHA-256 or SHA-3 for data integrity checks.
For digital signatures, use SHA-256 or stronger as the digest algorithm.
MD5 and SHA-1 are collision-vulnerable; avoid for any security-critical operation.
NIST formally deprecated SHA-1 for digital signatures in 2022 (NIST SP 800-131A Rev 2).
